prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
In the third turning of the fiscal wheel, by command of the High Steward of Coin (whom the people call CFO), I did enter the glass citadel and behold their ledgers, which glow like stained windows and hum with the spirits ARR and MRR. 
These twin household saints brought tithes aplenty, for subscriptions multiplied in the Cloud—an invisible kingdom residing in no sky I can point to—while the uptime oath of 99.9 parts in a hundred was kept as faithfully as any abbot at vigil, barring a brief incursion by the Gremlin of Latency upon the northern server farm. 
Some serfs did abscond—this they name churn—but the lords of Success galloped after with scrolls of onboarding and charms called SLAs, so that Net Revenue Retention rose to 118%, a number exceeding the whole and thus proof of miracles fit to rival fishes, loaves, and auto-scaling. 
As for the wars of acquisition, they spend CAC in coin per click to snare prospects into a funnel that is not a horn for ale, and by sacred calculus LTV surpasseth CAC threefold, wherefore the Board (a silent monastic order who meet by crystal) bids the product guild ship to sail the Q4 roadmap, though none can show me the sea upon which these features are shipped.
